channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17936] racer test_1: hang in mdt_reint_rename vs. mdt_object_local_lock with ldlm_completion_ast
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for sarah <sarah@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/619bf499-6edf-42fe-8984-958c0273b87f

test_1 failed with the following error:

Timeout occurred after 711 minutes, last suite running was racer

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-b2_15/87 - 5.14.21-150500.55.39-default
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-b2_15/87 - 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

client dmesg

[Fri May 31 18:55:06 2024] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl mark == racer test 1: racer on clients: onyx-75vm4,onyx-75vm5 DURATION=900 ========================================================== 18:55:07 \(1717181707\)
[Fri May 31 18:55:06 2024] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == racer test 1: racer on clients: onyx-75vm4,onyx-75vm5 DURATION=900 ========================================================== 18:55:07 (1717181707)
[Fri May 31 18:55:06 2024] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: DURATION=900 			MDSCOUNT=1 OSTCOUNT=7			RACER_ENABLE_REMOTE_DIRS=false 			RACER_ENABLE_STRIPED_DIRS=false 			RACER_ENABLE_MIGRATION=false 			RACER_ENABLE_PFL=true 			RACER_ENABLE_DOM=true 			RACER_ENABLE_FLR=true 			RACER_MAX_CLEANUP_WAIT= 			RACER_ENABLE
[Fri May 31 18:55:06 2024] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: DURATION=900 			MDSCOUNT=1 OSTCOUNT=7			RACER_ENABLE_REMOTE_DIRS=false 			RACER_ENABLE_STRIPED_DIRS=false 			RACER_ENABLE_MIGRATION=false 			RACER_ENABLE_PFL=true 			RACER_ENABLE_DOM=true 			RACER_ENABLE_FLR=true 			RACER_MAX_CLEANUP_WAIT= 			RACER_ENABLE
[Fri May 31 18:55:08 2024] 11[30738]: segfault at 8 ip 00007fadfa9754e8 sp 00007ffe9ed169a0 error 4 in ld-2.31.so[7fadfa968000+2a000]
[Fri May 31 18:55:08 2024] Code: 85 4c 12 00 00 49 83 ba f0 00 00 00 00 48 c7 85 10 ff ff ff 00 00 00 00 0f 85 f2 10 00 00 49 8b 42 68 49 83 ba f8 00 00 00 00 <48> 8b 40 08 48 89 85 30 ff ff ff 0f 84 77 03 00 00 45 85 ed 0f 85
[Fri May 31 18:55:08 2024] systemd-coredump[30819]: Not enough arguments passed by the kernel (0, expected 7).
[Fri May 31 18:55:09 2024] 2[31099]: segfault at 8 ip 00007fc03af404e8 sp 00007ffc7cd120c0 error 4 in ld-2.31.so[7fc03af33000+2a000]
[Fri May 31 18:55:09 2024] Code: 85 4c 12 00 00 49 83 ba f0 00 00 00 00 48 c7 85 10 ff ff ff 00 00 00 00 0f 85 f2 10 00 00 49 8b 42 68 49 83 ba f8 00 00 00 00 <48> 8b 40 08 48 89 85 30 ff ff ff 0f 84 77 03 00 00 45 85 ed 0f 85
[Fri May 31 18:55:09 2024] LustreError: 30738:0:(file.c:242:ll_close_inode_openhandle()) lustre-clilmv-ffff9eb2697ae800: inode [0x2000301a1:0x2d:0x0] mdc close failed: rc = -13
[Fri May 31 18:55:09 2024] systemd-coredump[31170]: Not enough arguments passed by the kernel (0, expected 7).
[Fri May 31 18:55:09 2024] 7[31390]: segfault at 8 ip 00007fe40ee4b4e8 sp 00007ffe1187a8c0 error 4 in ld-2.31.so[7fe40ee3e000+2a000]
[Fri May 31 18:55:09 2024] Code: 85 4c 12 00 00 49 83 ba f0 00 00 00 00 48 c7 85 10 ff ff ff 00 00 00 00 0f 85 f2 10 00 00 49 8b 42 68 49 83 ba f8 00 00 00 00 <48> 8b 40 08 48 89 85 30 ff ff ff 0f 84 77 03 00 00 45 85 ed 0f 85
[Fri May 31 18:55:09 2024] systemd-coredump[31442]: Not enough arguments passed by the kernel (0, expected 7).
[Fri May 31 18:55:16 2024] 6[6950]: segfault at 8 ip 00007f872bca14e8 sp 00007ffe59640b50 error 4 in ld-2.31.so[7f872bc94000+2a000]
[Fri May 31 18:55:16 2024] Code: 85 4c 12 00 00 49 83 ba f0 00 00 00 00 48 c7 85 10 ff ff ff 00 00 00 00 0f 85 f2 10 00 00 49 8b 42 68 49 83 ba f8 00 00 00 00 <48> 8b 40 08 48 89 85 30 ff ff ff 0f 84 77 03 00 00 45 85 ed 0f 85
[Fri May 31 18:55:16 2024] systemd-coredump[7076]: Not enough arguments passed by the kernel (0, expected 7).
[Fri May 31 18:55:16 2024] LustreError: 6950:0:(file.c:242:ll_close_inode_openhandle()) lustre-clilmv-ffff9eb2697ae800: inode [0x2000301a4:0x2a6:0x0] mdc close failed: rc = -13
[Fri May 31 18:55:16 2024] LustreError: 6950:0:(file.c:242:ll_close_inode_openhandle()) Skipped 1 previous similar message
[Fri May 31 18:55:16 2024] 6[7325]: segfault at 8 ip 00007f35830d84e8 sp 00007ffddfd813e0 error 4 in ld-2.31.so[7f35830cb000+2a000]
[Fri May 31 18:55:16 2024] Code: 85 4c 12 00 00 49 83 ba f0 00 00 00 00 48 c7 85 10 ff ff ff 00 00 00 00 0f 85 f2 10 00 00 49 8b 42 68 49 83 ba f8 00 00 00 00 <48> 8b 40 08 48 89 85 30 ff ff ff 0f 84 77 03 00 00 45 85 ed 0f 85
[Fri May 31 18:55:16 2024] systemd-coredump[7404]: Not enough arguments passed by the kernel (0, expected 7).

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
racer test_1 - Timeout occurred after 711 minutes, last suite running was racer
item.summary: racer test_1: hang in mdt_reint_rename vs. mdt_object_local_lock with ldlm_completion_ast

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
Lustre 2.15.5 client with 2.16.0.RC5 server: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/bac8f0fa-f5a5-4211-a7f0-f91d7f35774e

[13151.762457] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity test 422: kill a process with RPC in progress == 07:15:55 (1730013355)
[13198.197933] Lustre: mdt00_001: service thread pid 575158 was inactive for 42.596 seconds. The thread might be hung, or it might only be slow and will resume later. Dumping the stack trace for debugging purposes:
[13198.201988] Pid: 575158, comm: mdt00_001 4.18.0-553.16.1.el8_lustre.x86_64 #1 SMP Sun Oct 27 00:36:50 UTC 2024
[13198.204000] Call Trace TBD:
[13198.204711] [<0>] __cfs_fail_timeout_set+0xf7/0x1b0 [libcfs]
[13198.205897] [<0>] mdd_rename+0x378/0x1f60 [mdd]
[13198.206988] [<0>] mdt_reint_rename+0x11f2/0x1ed0 [mdt]
[13198.208297] [<0>] mdt_reint_rec+0x123/0x270 [mdt]
[13198.209305] [<0>] mdt_reint_internal+0x4b9/0x810 [mdt]
[13198.210409] [<0>] mdt_reint+0x5d/0x110 [mdt]
[13198.211372] [<0>] tgt_request_handle+0x3f4/0x1a30 [ptlrpc]
[13198.212628] [<0>] ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x2aa/0xcf0 [ptlrpc]
[13198.214003] [<0>] ptlrpc_main+0xc9e/0x15c0 [ptlrpc]
[13198.215129] [<0>] kthread+0x134/0x150
[13198.215925] [<0>] ret_from_fork+0x35/0x40
[13202.613817] LustreError: 575158:0:(mdd_dir.c:3272:mdd_rename()) cfs_fail_timeout id 722 awake
[13202.615842] Lustre: mdt00_001: service thread pid 575158 completed after 47.014s. This likely indicates the system was overloaded (too many service threads, or not enough hardware resources).

This doesn't look like a rename deadlock, the thread eventually completed, and was not in racer anyway.

+1 on master branch: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/985f633c-1919-4571-b993-0289b7bf2676

This doesn't look like a rename deadlock, since the rename finished and it wasn't in racer:

[13989.320536] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity test 422: kill a process with RPC in progress == 04:06:22 (1726459582)
[14034.189001] Lustre: mdt_io00_001: service thread pid 703553 was inactive for 41.245 seconds. The thread might be hung, or it might only be slow and will resume later. Dumping the stack trace for debugging purposes:
[14034.192459] Pid: 703553, comm: mdt_io00_001 4.18.0-513.24.1.el8_lustre.x86_64 #1 SMP Sun Aug 25 16:53:08 UTC 2024
[14034.194264] Call Trace TBD:
[14034.194920] [<0>] __cfs_fail_timeout_set+0xf7/0x1b0 [libcfs]
[14034.195984] [<0>] mdd_rename+0x378/0x1f60 [mdd]
[14034.196896] [<0>] mdt_reint_rename+0x11f2/0x1ed0 [mdt]
[14034.198009] [<0>] mdt_reint_rec+0x123/0x270 [mdt]
[14034.198900] [<0>] mdt_reint_internal+0x4b9/0x810 [mdt]
[14034.199862] [<0>] mdt_reint+0x5d/0x110 [mdt]
[14034.200687] [<0>] tgt_request_handle+0x3f4/0x1a30 [ptlrpc]
[14034.202113] [<0>] ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x2aa/0xcf0 [ptlrpc]
[14034.203318] [<0>] ptlrpc_main+0xc9e/0x15c0 [ptlrpc]
[14034.204289] [<0>] kthread+0x134/0x150
[14034.205020] [<0>] ret_from_fork+0x35/0x40
[14039.956963] LustreError: 703553:0:(mdd_dir.c:3292:mdd_rename()) cfs_fail_timeout id 722 awake
[14039.959015] Lustre: mdt_io00_001: service thread pid 703553 completed after 47.015s. This likely indicates the system was overloaded (too many service threads, or not enough hardware resources).

racer lockups are unfortunately relatively common. It's not a given here we have a rename deadlock and not some other lock that then is preventing rename from running.

We really need to add maloo functionality to crashdump timed out tests for further examination - that will give us all the lock information and much more.

Oleg

Can you please see what can/should be done here?

Peter

It looks like there are MDT stack traces stuck in rename:

[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024] task:mdt00_029       state:S stack:    0 pid:2635837 ppid:     2 flags:0x80004080
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024] Call Trace:
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  __schedule+0x2d1/0x870
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  schedule+0x55/0xf0
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  ldlm_completion_ast+0x57e/0x900 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  ldlm_cli_enqueue_local+0x30b/0x870 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  mdt_object_local_lock+0x50e/0xb30 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  mdt_object_lock_internal+0x191/0x4a0 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  mdt_reint_object_lock+0x27/0x60 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  mdt_reint_rename+0x1829/0x1d40 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  mdt_reint_rec+0x11f/0x270 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  mdt_reint_internal+0x4d0/0x7e0 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  mdt_reint+0x5d/0x110 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  tgt_request_handle+0xccd/0x1b10 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x323/0xbe0 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 20:25:53 2024]  ptlrpc_main+0xc0f/0x1560 [ptlrpc]

but we don't have a good way to dump the DLM lock state to see what these threads are stuck on. Something like LU-16375 or LU-14858 would help here.

MDS dmesg

[Fri May 31 18:55:07 2024] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl mark == racer test 1: racer on clients: onyx-75vm4,onyx-75vm5 DURATION=900 ========================================================== 18:55:07 \(1717181707\)
[Fri May 31 18:55:07 2024] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == racer test 1: racer on clients: onyx-75vm4,onyx-75vm5 DURATION=900 ========================================================== 18:55:07 (1717181707)
[Fri May 31 18:55:11 2024] Lustre: 2635833:0:(mdt_recovery.c:200:mdt_req_from_lrd()) @@@ restoring transno  req@0000000091d03599 x1800577145701056/t425201770320(0) o101->e7093888-02d0-4930-a805-bd0eab886778@10.240.26.79@tcp:658/0 lens 384/888 e 0 to 0 dl 1717181718 ref 1 fl Interpret:H/2/0 rc 0/0 job:'dd.0'
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Lustre: mdt00_012: service thread pid 2635820 was inactive for 42.780 seconds. The thread might be hung, or it might only be slow and will resume later. Dumping the stack trace for debugging purposes:
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Pid: 2635830, comm: mdt00_022 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu May 30 22:41:31 UTC 2024
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Lustre: Skipped 1 previous similar message
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Call Trace TBD:
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_completion_ast+0x7b0/0x900 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_cli_enqueue_local+0x30b/0x870 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_object_local_lock+0x50e/0xb30 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_object_lock_internal+0x191/0x4a0 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_getattr_name_lock+0x8f3/0x2380 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_intent_getattr+0x27e/0x470 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_intent_opc+0x12c/0xbf0 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_intent_policy+0x20b/0x3a0 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_lock_enqueue+0x4e8/0xaa0 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_handle_enqueue0+0x634/0x1750 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] tgt_enqueue+0xa4/0x220 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] tgt_request_handle+0xccd/0x1b10 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x323/0xbe0 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ptlrpc_main+0xc0f/0x1560 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] kthread+0x134/0x150
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ret_from_fork+0x35/0x40
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Pid: 2635820, comm: mdt00_012 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu May 30 22:41:31 UTC 2024
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Call Trace TBD:
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_completion_ast+0x7b0/0x900 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_cli_enqueue_local+0x30b/0x870 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_object_local_lock+0x50e/0xb30 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_object_lock_internal+0x191/0x4a0 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_getattr_name_lock+0x8f3/0x2380 [mdt]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] mdt_intent_getattr+0x27e/0x470 [mdt]

[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_lock_enqueue+0x4e8/0xaa0 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_handle_enqueue0+0x634/0x1750 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] tgt_enqueue+0xa4/0x220 [ptlrpc]

[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x323/0xbe0 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ptlrpc_main+0xc0f/0x1560 [ptlrpc]
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] kthread+0x134/0x150
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ret_from_fork+0x35/0x40
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Pid: 2635824, comm: mdt00_016 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu May 30 22:41:31 UTC 2024
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Lustre: mdt00_006: service thread pid 2635814 was inactive for 42.826 seconds. Watchdog stack traces are limited to 3 per 300 seconds, skipping this one.
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] Call Trace TBD:
[Fri May 31 18:56:02 2024] [<0>] ldlm_completion_ast+0x7b0/0x900 [ptlrpc]
